content.stem: Topic Discussion — Talk about technology and social media.
content.prompt: The examiner will ask you questions about technology and social media. Give your opinions and support them with reasons and examples from your own experience.
examiner_questions: How often do you use social media? What platforms do you use?
Do you think social media is a good thing for young people? Why or why not?
Some people say that teenagers spend too much time on their phones. Do you agree?
How has technology changed the way you learn at school?
content.sample_answer: I use social media quite often — probably about an hour a day. I mainly use WeChat to talk to my friends and Bilibili to watch videos. I think social media can be good for young people because it helps us stay in touch with friends and learn new things. However, it can also be a problem if you spend too much time on it. I partly agree that teenagers use their phones too much. Sometimes I notice my friends checking their phones during meals, which isn't very polite. Technology has really changed how I learn — for example, I can watch educational videos online and use apps to practice English. It makes learning more interesting and convenient.
content.tips_zh: PET话题讨论要求你就较复杂的社会话题发表看法。技巧：1）先明确表态（I think... / In my opinion...）；2）给出理由（because... / The reason is...）；3）举例说明（For example... / For instance...）；4）可以表达不同角度的观点（On the other hand... / However...）。不要害怕表达自己的真实想法，考官关注的是你的语言能力，不是观点本身。

explanation_zh: 这是PET口语第四部分——话题讨论。考官会围绕一个社会话题问你问题，你需要表达观点、给出理由并举例。这部分考查你讨论较复杂话题的能力。
explanation_en: This is PET Speaking Part 4 — Topic Discussion. The examiner asks questions around a social topic, and you need to express opinions, give reasons, and provide examples. This part tests your ability to discuss more complex topics.

content.stem: Topic Discussion — Talk about education and learning.
content.prompt: The examiner will ask you questions about education and learning. Give your opinions and support them with reasons and examples from your own experience.
examiner_questions: What do you think makes a good teacher?
Do you think homework is important? Why or why not?
Some people believe that exams are the best way to test students. Do you agree?
How do you think schools could be improved?
Do you think online learning is as good as learning in a classroom?
content.sample_answer: I think a good teacher should be patient, enthusiastic, and able to explain things in different ways so that everyone can understand. Homework is quite important because it helps you practise what you've learned, but I don't think teachers should give too much — students need time to relax too. I partly agree about exams — they're useful for testing knowledge, but they can be very stressful, and some students are just bad at exams even though they understand the subject well. I think schools could be improved by having more practical lessons and fewer lectures. Online learning is convenient, but I don't think it's as good as being in a classroom because you miss the interaction with your teacher and classmates.
content.tips_zh: 讨论教育话题时，可以结合自己的学习经历来支持观点。谈到好老师的品质时，列举具体特征并解释为什么重要。对于有争议的问题（如作业和考试），可以给出平衡的观点（partly agree）。使用 "I think... should be..."、"I partly agree because..." 和 "I don't think... is as good as..." 等结构表达复杂观点。

explanation_zh: 这是PET口语第四部分关于教育和学习的话题讨论。你需要讨论好老师的品质、作业的重要性、考试制度、学校改进和在线学习。
explanation_en: This is PET Speaking Part 4 about education and learning. You need to discuss what makes a good teacher, the importance of homework, exams, school improvements, and online learning.

content.stem: Topic Discussion — Talk about the environment and climate change.
content.prompt: The examiner will ask you questions about the environment and climate change. Give your opinions and support them with reasons and examples from your own experience.
examiner_questions: Do you think enough is being done to protect the environment? Why or why not?
What can ordinary people do in their daily lives to help the environment?
Some people think that governments should spend more money on protecting the environment. Do you agree?
How do you think climate change will affect our lives in the future?
Do you think young people care more about the environment than older people?
content.sample_answer: I don't think enough is being done to protect the environment. Although some progress has been made, we still produce too much waste and use too much energy from fossil fuels. Ordinary people can help by recycling, using public transport instead of driving, and reducing the amount of plastic they use. I definitely agree that governments should spend more money on protecting the environment — they need to invest in renewable energy like solar and wind power. I think climate change will affect our lives in many ways, for example through more extreme weather events and rising sea levels. I think young people do care more about the environment because we've grown up learning about these problems and we know our future depends on it.
content.tips_zh: 讨论环境话题需要展示对时事的了解。表达观点时，从个人、社会和政府层面分析。提到具体的环保行动（recycling, renewable energy, public transport）让回答更有说服力。使用 "I don't think enough is being done" 和 "I definitely agree that..." 表达明确立场。谈到未来影响时，举出具体例子。

explanation_zh: 这是PET口语第四部分关于环境和气候变化的话题讨论。你需要讨论环保措施是否足够、个人能做什么、政府的责任、气候变化的影响以及年轻人和老年人的态度差异。
explanation_en: This is PET Speaking Part 4 about the environment and climate change. You need to discuss whether enough is being done, individual actions, government responsibility, future impacts, and generational differences in attitudes.

content.stem: Topic Discussion — Talk about health and wellbeing.
content.prompt: The examiner will ask you questions about health and wellbeing. Give your opinions and support them with reasons and examples from your own experience.
examiner_questions: What do you think is the most important thing people can do to stay healthy?
Do you think young people today have a healthy lifestyle? Why or why not?
Some people say that PE lessons should be compulsory every day at school. What do you think?
How important is mental health compared to physical health?
What advice would you give to someone who wants to live a healthier life?
content.sample_answer: I think the most important thing people can do to stay healthy is to exercise regularly and eat a balanced diet. Unfortunately, I don't think all young people today have a healthy lifestyle. Many of us spend too much time on our phones and computers, and some teenagers prefer fast food to home-cooked meals. I think having PE every day is a good idea in theory, but it might be difficult to fit into the school timetable. Maybe three or four times a week would be more realistic. I believe mental health is just as important as physical health — if you're feeling stressed or anxious, it can affect your body too. My advice for a healthier life would be to walk more, eat more fruit and vegetables, get enough sleep, and spend time with people who make you happy.
content.tips_zh: 讨论健康话题时，平衡身体健康和心理健康两个方面。谈到年轻人的生活方式时，可以承认存在的问题（too much screen time, fast food）。对于体育课的问题，可以给出中间立场（good idea but...）。给建议时，列举具体、可行的行动。使用 "I think... is just as important as..." 表达同等重要性，"My advice would be to..." 给出建议。

explanation_zh: 这是PET口语第四部分关于健康和幸福的话题讨论。你需要讨论保持健康的方法、年轻人的生活方式、体育课、心理健康和健康生活建议。
explanation_en: This is PET Speaking Part 4 about health and wellbeing. You need to discuss staying healthy, young people's lifestyles, PE lessons, mental health, and advice for living healthier.

content.stem: Topic Discussion — Talk about culture and traditions.
content.prompt: The examiner will ask you questions about culture and traditions. Give your opinions and support them with reasons and examples from your own experience.
examiner_questions: What is your favourite festival or celebration? Why do you enjoy it?
Do you think it's important to keep traditional customs alive? Why or why not?
How have celebrations and traditions changed over the years?
Do you think young people are interested in traditional culture? Why or why not?
What can we learn from other countries' cultures?
content.sample_answer: My favourite festival is Chinese New Year because the whole family gets together, we have a big dinner, and there are fireworks. It's such a happy time. I think it's very important to keep traditional customs alive because they connect us to our history and bring families together. However, celebrations have changed a lot — for example, nowadays people send digital red envelopes on WeChat instead of giving paper ones. I think some young people are interested in traditional culture, but many prefer modern entertainment. Schools should do more to teach students about traditions. We can learn a lot from other countries' cultures — for instance, I admire how people in Japan value politeness and respect. Learning about different cultures helps us understand each other better.
content.tips_zh: 讨论文化和传统时，分享你自己的文化体验和具体的节日例子。谈到传统的变化时，举出新旧对比的例子（如纸质红包vs电子红包）。讨论年轻人对传统文化的态度时，可以承认既有感兴趣的也有不感兴趣的。谈到不同文化时，展示开放和尊重的态度。使用 "I think it's important to... because..." 和 "For instance..." 等结构组织回答。

explanation_zh: 这是PET口语第四部分关于文化和传统的话题讨论。你需要讨论你最喜欢的节日、保持传统的重要性、庆祝方式的变化、年轻人的态度和跨文化学习。
explanation_en: This is PET Speaking Part 4 about culture and traditions. You need to discuss your favourite festival, the importance of keeping traditions, how celebrations have changed, young people's attitudes, and what we can learn from other cultures.

content.stem: Topic Discussion — Talk about social media and communication.
content.prompt: The examiner will ask you questions about social media and how people communicate. Give your opinions and support them with reasons and examples from your own experience.
examiner_questions: How do you usually communicate with your friends and family?
Do you think social media has changed the way people make friends? How?
Some people think that face-to-face communication is better than online communication. Do you agree?
Do you think there are any dangers of using social media? What are they?
How do you think people will communicate in the future?
content.sample_answer: I usually communicate with my friends through WeChat and sometimes by phone. With my family, I prefer talking face to face. I think social media has definitely changed how people make friends — you can now connect with people from all over the world, which is amazing. However, online friendships aren't always as deep as real-life ones. I agree that face-to-face communication is better because you can see the person's expressions and body language, which helps you understand them better. There are some dangers of social media, like cyberbullying and sharing too much personal information. Some people also compare themselves to others online, which can make them feel unhappy. In the future, I think people might use virtual reality to communicate — it would feel like being in the same room even though you're far apart.
content.tips_zh: 讨论社交媒体和沟通方式时，从个人经验出发，但也要考虑更广泛的社会影响。比较线上和线下沟通时，分析各自的优缺点。讨论危险时，举出具体例子（cyberbullying, privacy issues）。谈到未来时，可以发挥想象力但要合理。使用 "I agree that... because..." 和 "However..." 展示平衡的观点。

explanation_zh: 这是PET口语第四部分关于社交媒体和沟通方式的话题讨论。你需要讨论你的沟通方式、社交媒体对交友的影响、面对面沟通vs在线沟通、社交媒体的危险以及未来的沟通方式。
explanation_en: This is PET Speaking Part 4 about social media and communication. You need to discuss how you communicate, social media's impact on friendships, face-to-face vs online communication, dangers of social media, and future communication methods.

content.stem: Topic Discussion — Talk about travel and exploring new places.
content.prompt: The examiner will ask you questions about travel and exploring new places. Give your opinions and support them with reasons and examples from your own experience.
examiner_questions: Why do you think people like to travel?
Do you think travelling to other countries is important for young people? Why?
Some people prefer to stay in hotels, while others prefer camping or hostels. What do you prefer and why?
How has tourism changed the way people see the world?
Do you think tourism can be bad for some places? Why or why not?
content.sample_answer: I think people like to travel because it's a chance to relax, see beautiful places, and experience different cultures. For young people, I think travelling to other countries is really important because it opens your mind and helps you become more independent. You also learn to communicate with people from different backgrounds. I prefer staying in hostels because they're cheaper than hotels and you can meet other travellers. My parents prefer hotels, though, because they're more comfortable! Tourism has made the world feel smaller — people now know much more about other countries because they can visit them easily. However, I think tourism can be bad for some places. Popular tourist spots can become very crowded, and sometimes local culture and nature get damaged. We should try to be responsible travellers.
content.tips_zh: 讨论旅行话题时，从个人偏好扩展到更广泛的社会影响。解释人们旅行的动机时，列举多个原因（relaxation, culture, independence）。比较住宿方式时，可以提到不同人的不同偏好。讨论旅游的积极和消极影响时，保持平衡。使用 "I think... because..." 和 "However..." 展示多角度思考。

explanation_zh: 这是PET口语第四部分关于旅行和探索新地方的话题讨论。你需要讨论人们为什么喜欢旅行、旅行对年轻人的重要性、住宿偏好、旅游如何改变世界认知以及旅游的负面影响。
explanation_en: This is PET Speaking Part 4 about travel and exploring new places. You need to discuss why people like to travel, the importance of travel for young people, accommodation preferences, how tourism has changed perspectives, and the negative impacts of tourism.

content.stem: Topic Discussion — Talk about the future of work and careers.
content.prompt: The examiner will ask you questions about work and careers in the future. Give your opinions and support them with reasons and examples from your own experience.
examiner_questions: What kind of jobs do you think will be most important in the future?
Do you think robots and artificial intelligence will replace many jobs? Which ones?
Some people think that it's more important to have a job you enjoy than a job that pays well. Do you agree?
How do you think the way people work will change in the next twenty years?
What skills do you think students should learn now to prepare for future jobs?
content.sample_answer: I think jobs in technology, healthcare, and environmental science will be the most important in the future because these are areas that are growing quickly. I do think robots and AI will replace some jobs, especially repetitive ones like factory work or simple data entry. However, I don't think they'll replace jobs that need creativity or human connection, like teaching or counselling. I agree that it's more important to enjoy your job — if you hate your work, you won't be happy even if you earn a lot of money. In the next twenty years, I think more people will work from home and have flexible hours. Companies might use virtual reality for meetings instead of travelling. Students should learn digital skills like coding, but also soft skills like communication and teamwork, because these will always be important no matter how much technology changes.
content.tips_zh: 讨论未来工作话题时，展示你对科技发展和社会变化的思考。谈到AI和机器人时，区分可能被替代的工作和不会被替代的工作。讨论工作满意度vs薪资时，给出你的立场和理由。谈到未来技能时，平衡技术技能和软技能。使用 "I think... will be..." 预测未来，"I agree that..." 表达立场，"no matter how much..." 等复杂句型展示语言能力。

explanation_zh: 这是PET口语第四部分关于未来工作和职业的话题讨论。你需要讨论未来最重要的工作、AI对就业的影响、工作满意度vs薪资、工作方式的变化以及学生应该学习的技能。
explanation_en: This is PET Speaking Part 4 about the future of work and careers. You need to discuss important future jobs, AI's impact on employment, job satisfaction vs pay, changes in working methods, and skills students should learn.
